summary.title: Launch Astro website for Mainline
summary.what: Built a standalone Astro website repo for Mainline with a product landing page, docs overview, spec overview, comparison SEO pages, a Markdown blog index, the first repo-memory essay, static social assets, sitemap support, Vercel config, and updated repository README.
summary.why: Mainline needs a separate public web presence for polished landing copy, blog publishing, SEO pages, docs entry points, and design-partner conversion without mixing website churn into the product CLI repository.
summary.user_goal: Initialize the independent mainline-org/website repository with Mainline, build the website from the main product README and supplied essay, push it, and publish it to Vercel.

decisions.chose: Use Astro with Markdown content and static output.
decisions.rationale: The site is content-heavy, SEO-oriented, and currently needs landing, docs, spec, comparison, and blog pages more than an application runtime.

decisions.chose: Keep the website repo separate from the Mainline product repo.
decisions.rationale: Website copy, blog, design, analytics, and deployment cadence can change frequently without polluting CLI and protocol release history.

decisions.chose: Use usemainline.dev as the configured canonical site URL before the domain is registered.
decisions.rationale: Canonical URLs and sitemap output can be correct for the intended domain while Vercel provides the temporary deployment URL.

decisions.chose: Disable Astro telemetry in npm scripts.
decisions.rationale: The local sandbox blocks Astro's default preferences write, and disabling telemetry keeps builds deterministic in this environment.

decisions.chose: Frame Mainline as pre-edit intent memory, not enforcement magic.
decisions.rationale: The copy repeatedly explains that agents read anti-patterns, risks, decisions, and constraints before editing; this mitigates prior concern that 'stop agents' language could overpromise enforcement.

rejected.alternative: Put the website inside the main product repository.
rejected.reason: The user explicitly preferred an independent website repo, and the release cadence differs from the CLI and protocol.

rejected.alternative: Use Next.js for the first version.
rejected.reason: The current site is static and content-led; Astro is lighter and better aligned with the SEO/blog goal.

rejected.alternative: Make the blog canonical on Medium first.
rejected.reason: The supplied publishing strategy prioritizes the owned domain as canonical source for SEO.

risks: Hero copy still uses 'Stop coding agents...' language; although the page explains the mechanism as pre-edit retrieval of recorded intent, reviewers should keep watching for copy that implies stronger enforcement than the product provides.
followups: Register and bind the production domain usemainline.dev when available.

acknowledged_constraints.note: The inherited constraint is about substring matching in eval scoring; this website work did not implement or rely on eval scoring.

review_notes: Verified with npm run build, which ran astro check and astro build successfully.
npm install reported 5 moderate audit findings; no automatic audit fix was applied because that could change the dependency tree before launch.

architectural_claims: The website is a standalone Astro static site independent of the Mainline product repository.
The site canonical URL is configured as https://usemainline.dev while deployment can use a temporary Vercel URL.
Markdown is used for long-form blog content.
behavioral_changes: Root path now renders a Mainline landing page.
Docs, spec, blog, and comparison routes are available as static pages.
Astro build emits a sitemap and static assets for deployment.
migration_notes: Replaced the placeholder website README with Astro project documentation.
Initialized Mainline configuration in the website repo.
